name: Refa Straight Iron Pro
pros: Dual carbon-coated plates deliver salon-quality results with minimal hair damage
Works globally (100-240V) — ideal for expats moving between countries
Consistently ranked #1 on Rakuten with near-perfect 4.61★ rating from nearly 2,000 reviews
cons: ¥23,000 is steep compared to budget irons; justified only if you prioritize hair health
Instructions are Japanese-only; online video guides help but may frustrate non-speakers
shortDescription: Premium ceramic hair straightener with dual heating plates and global voltage compatibility.
review: The Refa Pro has earned its Rakuten top spot for a reason — it glides through hair with minimal frizz and heat damage, thanks to MTG's carbon-coating technology. At ¥23,000, it's an investment, but if you're in Japan long-term and care about hair quality, this beats most drugstore alternatives. The dual voltage is a genuine plus for expats who travel. Note: instructions are Japanese-only, though the device itself is intuitive.
